meta.voice: Brendan Franks data-first. Engagement tweets are FRESH posts that tag 1-3 high-value AZ accounts with a data point, primary-source citation, and an offer or question. Different from x-mentions.json (those were single-account broadcast). These read like a peer journalist sliding into the conversation.
meta.rules: Under 280 chars. Lead with the number / named entity. The @ tags appear naturally in the sentence, not stacked at the start. End with an offer to share records OR a specific question that invites reply. Reply tweet has the link.

posts.text:
The APS / Korman $7M settlement reinstates the 95-degree heat hold. ACC Commissioner Nick Myers, on record at @KJZZPhoenix: "I refuse to tell utilities they have to provide power to people that do not pay their bills."

Three days. 99 degrees. Six days later, dead.
posts.reply:
We have the AG release, the ME findings, and the named ACC vote breakdown. Dossier is publication-ready. Reach out for the records.

cc @AZAGMayes @AARPArizona

posts.text:
ASU Preparatory Academy posted a $5.46M operating loss on $82.4M revenue last fiscal year. The FY21 audit at @ADHS_AZ filings discloses a related-party building lease. $11.5M ROU. Through September 2032.

Note 9 of the audit names the counterparty.
posts.reply:
Full breakdown with the audit text + ARS 39-121 public-records path:

azlawnow.com/investigations/asu-prep-related-party-lease/

cc @SOSArizona @ArizonaEA @AZAuditor

posts.text:
DOJ-EDNY Walgreens settlement: $350M. Covered conduct: August 2012 through March 2023. Arizona's most recent 12-month opioid death count: 1,659.

Which Arizona Walgreens stores filled the prescriptions covered by the settlement? The list sits in the EDNY federal docket.
posts.reply:
We mapped the AZ regulator chain, the statutory frame, and the records that close the gap:

azlawnow.com/investigations/walgreens-350m-arizona-opioid-stores/

cc @ShatterproofHQ @AZAGMayes @MaricopaPH

posts.text:
The Mayes / DHS / ICE complaint is the fourth state lawsuit on this fact pattern in 2026. Maryland TRO landed March 11. NJ March 20. Michigan March 24. Arizona April 24.

The Eleventh Circuit's Friends of the Everglades opinion is the test. Arizona meets it cleanly.
posts.reply:
We mapped the NEPA pleading, the Rinchem chemical zone (chlorine, hydrofluoric acid, fluorine), and the sister-state precedent:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-mayes-ice-surprise-detention-lawsuit/

cc @AZAGMayes @ACLUArizona @FIRRPAZ

posts.text:
Arizona school districts bought MERV 13 filters and called the air hospital-grade. ASHRAE 52.2 tests the filter media in a sealed apparatus, not the housing seal in the field. ASHRAE 241-2023 says air bypassing an unsealed filter performs as a lower MERV.

No AZ district publishes PM2.5.
posts.reply:
We pulled the standards text + the EPA NAAQS tightening + the Maricopa PM10 non-attainment context:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-schools-merv-13-filter-bypass/

cc @SOSArizona @AzPTAofficial @ASHRAEnews

posts.text:
AZ State Board of Education adjudicated 325 educator disciplinary actions in 2024. A 491% increase from 2012. Sexual misconduct: 36% of 1,876 cases since 2012. Maricopa County drove 55.6% of cases Jan 2023 to Dec 2024.

Voluntary surrender is the dominant outcome at 39%.
posts.reply:
Full data + ARS 12-514 12-year SOL + ARS 12-821.01 180-day NOC clocks + six theories of district recovery:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-325-educator-discipline-2024/

cc @ArizonaEA @SOSArizona @AzPTAofficial

posts.text:
Arizona's pedestrian fatality rate runs nearly 2x the national average. The worst corridors map cleanly against ADOT and MAG road-design decisions. FHWA Proven Safety Countermeasures show what redesigns work.

Which AZ cities have picked them up? The data answers that.
posts.reply:
We mapped the corridors against the design decisions:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-pedestrian-deaths-road-design/

cc @StreetsblogUSA @StrongTowns @VisionZeroUS @AZDeptOfTrans

posts.text:
60% of nursing-home sexual-abuse victims have dementia. 80% of perpetrators are caregivers. Only 30% of incidents reach law enforcement.

A Mesa Grand Court Senior Living staffer was arrested April 21, 2026. Family caught it on video. HB2228 is moving in the AZ Senate.
